label: 397_nafta_mexico_canada mexico_north american
description: The us, canada, and mexico negotiated the north american free trade agreement, but the trump administration sought major changes. The us objectives included stronger intellectual property protections and better labor standards, while both canada and mexico aimed to preserve the deal's benefits.

central_articles.title: Scenarios for Concluding the NAFTA Talks
central_articles.excerpt: Canada and Mexico want to conclude the renegotiation of the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) by December 2017, but their hopes are likely to be frustrated. A main reason is that the US negotiating objectives, announced by the US Trade Representative (USTR) on July 17, are bound to trigger strong opposition…
